mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing the present value of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing equity risk premiums to widen and S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold being breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer price inflation and decreases in the federal funds rate compress real interest rates into negative territory, prompting investors to rotate into equities as a hedge against currency debasement and driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly decline in new housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a collapsing construction sector and deteriorating household balance sheets, which transmits to broader corporate earnings and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts drop below threshold but SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 55% of cases, indicating decoupling from housing cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and higher capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the short term.
failure_condition: Rank IC for industrial production turns negative over consecutive 12-month windows, or forward returns show no statistical correlation with production growth.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal consumption expenditures exceed a 3-month moving average threshold of 370,000 million dollars, robust consumer spending sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal triggers but SPX 20-day returns are negative in > 60% of instances, indicating consumer spending is no longer a reliable earnings driver.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction effect vanishes when tested against a simple average of the two series, with combined signal IC < 0.01 over walk-forward periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth deceleration below 2.0 percent during an established expansion signals an impending economic slowdown, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in lower earnings.
failure_condition: GDP growth slows below threshold but equity markets rally, with 60-day forward returns positive in > 70% of triggered observations.
